item.title: AutoZone第二季度利润下滑，通胀逆风影响
item.description: AutoZone周二公布第二季度利润下滑，通胀逆风削弱了这家汽车零部件零售商的利润率。【原文：AutoZone reported a fall in second-quarter profit on Tuesday, as ​inflationary headwinds dented the auto parts ‌retailer's margins.】

item.title: 分析师称，油价冲击可能使新兴市场面临通胀之外的压力
item.description: 分析师警告称，伊朗战争及其引发的能源价格飙升，对新兴市场的影响将远不止于通胀，还将对外部收支平衡、货币汇率及资本流动造成更广泛的压力。【原文：The war in Iran and the resulting surge in energy prices will impact emerging markets well beyond inflation to broader pressures on external ​balances, currencies and capital flows, analysts warn.】

item.title: 美联储降息疑虑升温，中东冲突推高能源价格
item.description: 交易员正下调对美联储今年降息的预期，因他们担忧能源价格飙升带来的通胀效应将使央行货币政策路径复杂化。【原文：Traders are paring back their expectations on interest-rate cuts this year by the U.S. Federal Reserve, as they fear that the inflationary ​effects of a surge in energy prices will complicate the central bank's ‌monetary policy path.】

item.title: 挪威主权财富基金首次投资美国可再生能源资产
item.description: 挪威主权财富基金——全球规模最大的主权财富基金——周二宣布，已首次在美国可再生能源资产领域进行投资，收购了17座太阳能电站和5座陆上风电场组合的33.3%股权。【原文：Norway's sovereign wealth fund, the ​world's largest, has made its first investment ‌in renewable energy assets in the United States, buying a 33.3% stake in a portfolio ​of 17 solar plants and five ​onshore wind power facilities, it said on Tuesday.】

item.title: Ziff Davis将以12亿美元价格向埃森哲出售网络连接业务部门
item.description: Ziff Davis周二宣布，已同意以12亿美元现金将旗下Connectivity部门——该部门运营Ookla、Speedtest和Downdetector等品牌——出售给埃森哲。【原文：Ziff Davis said on Tuesday ​it has agreed ‌to sell its Connectivity division — the unit ​behind brands such ​as Ookla, Speedtest and ⁠Downdetector — to Accenture ​for $1.2 billion in cash.】

item.title: Versant自康卡斯特分拆后首次公布财报，年度营收下滑5.3%
item.description: 维森特传媒周二公布了自从康卡斯特完成分拆后的首份财报，显示年度营收出现下滑，由此形成了一家以传统线性有线电视网络为主导的企业。【原文：Versant Media on Tuesday reported ​a fall in ‌annual revenue in the first ​results since ​the completion of ⁠its spin-off ​from Comcast, ​creating a business dominated by legacy ​linear ​cable networks.】

item.title: 激进投资者埃利奥特以10亿美元入股Pinterest
item.description: Pinterest周二表示，激进投资公司Elliott Investment Management将向该公司投资10亿美元。【原文：Pinterest ​said ‌on ​Tuesday ​activist ⁠investing ​firm ​Elliott Investment ​Management ​is investing $1 ‌billion ⁠in ​the ​company.】

item.title: 百思买假日季度盈利超预期，股价飙升
item.description: 百思买周二公布的第四季度利润超出华尔街预期，这家美国最大电子产品零售商通过削减成本实现盈利，尽管假日购物季期间游戏设备和手机的需求依然疲软。【原文：Best Buy beat Wall ​Street estimates for fourth-quarter profit ‌on Tuesday, aided by the top U.S. electronics retailer's efforts ​to cut costs as ​demand during the holiday shopping season ⁠for gaming devices and ​mobile phones remained muted.】

item.title: 伊朗危机扰乱航运及油气生产，全球能源成本飙升
item.description: 周二全球油气价格飙升，因美以对伊朗的战争导致中东能源出口中断——德黑兰袭击船只和能源设施，关闭波斯湾航道，迫使从卡塔尔到伊拉克的产油国停产。【原文：Global oil and gas prices jumped on Tuesday as the ​U.S.-Israeli war on Iran halted energy exports from the Middle East, with Tehran attacking ships and energy facilities, closing navigation in the Gulf ‌and forcing production stoppages from Qatar to Iraq.】

item.title: 塔吉特新任首席执行官以乐观展望宣告业务复苏
item.description: 塔吉特新任首席执行官迈克尔·菲德尔克承诺将恢复年度销售额增长，并发布了乐观的盈利预期，这标志着这家陷入困境的零售商正全力推动业务复苏。【原文：Target's new CEO Michael Fiddelke promised a return to annual sales growth and issued an upbeat profit outlook, ​signalling a turnaround push at the embattled retailer.】

item.title: 早盘竞价：霍尔木兹雾霾冲击市场
item.description: 当今美国及全球市场中真正重要的因素【原文：What matters in U.S. and global markets today】

item.title: 欧洲央行报告指出，稳定币的使用可能削弱欧洲央行的政策效力，并阻碍贷款机构的运作。
item.description: 欧洲央行周二发布的研究报告显示，稳定币在欧元区的扩散可能削弱货币政策的效力，导致银行存款流失，并减少对实体经济的贷款。【原文：The spread of stablecoins in the euro zone could weaken the effectiveness of monetary policy, siphon deposits away from banks and reduce lending ​to the real economy, a study published by the European Central ‌Bank showed on Tuesday.】

item.title: Realtor.com称，2025年美国住房供应缺口将进一步扩大
item.description: 一份独立报告周二显示，2025年美国住房供应缺口进一步扩大，同时新组建家庭数量增加，这意味着许多潜在购房者将继续被排除在市场之外。【原文：The U.S. housing supply gap widened further in 2025 while more households were formed, an independent report showed on Tuesday, suggesting that many potential homeowners will remain sidelined from ​the market.】

item.title: Ticketmaster与Live Nation的反垄断诉讼案在纽约开庭
item.description: 票务巨头Ticketmaster及其母公司Live Nation将于周二出庭受审，美国政府指控这家娱乐集团通过垄断现场演出市场损害了艺术家、场馆和乐迷的利益。【原文：Ticketmaster and its parent company Live Nation are set to face trial on Tuesday over U.S. claims that ​the entertainment conglomerate dominated live event markets in ways that ‌hurt artists, venues and fans.】

item.title: 软件行业暴跌后，回购计划不足以安抚投资者
item.description: 在持续数月的股价暴跌中，美国软件公司纷纷扩大股票回购计划。投资者和策略师对此能否遏制抛售潮持怀疑态度。【原文：U.S. software companies have stepped up their stock buyback plans during a months-long rout. Investors and strategists are skeptical that ​it will stem the selling.】

item.title: 寻求关税退还者涌向一家鲜为人知却处理过重大案件的美国法院
item.description: 寻求分得逾1300亿美元关税退税份额的进口商正蜂拥至一家鲜为人知的美国贸易法庭，该法庭如今必须设法应对预计将激增的案件量。【原文：Importers seeking their share of more than $130 billion in tariff refunds are flocking to a little-known U.S. trade court, which must now figure out how to deal ​with what is expected to be an explosion of cases.】

item.title: 运动品牌On有望受益于美国降低关税税率
item.description: 首席执行官马丁·霍夫曼周二在公布强劲季度业绩时表示，随着美国最高法院推翻政府紧急关税措施，On Holding预计美国关税税率将下调，这可能为这家快速发展的瑞士运动品牌带来提振。【原文：On Holding sees lower U.S. tariff rates after the ‌Supreme Court struck down the government's emergency levies as a possible boost for the fast-growing Swiss sportswear brand, CEO Martin Hoffmann said on Tuesday as it delivered strong quarterly results.】

item.title: Stellantis创新主管力求在战略重置期间保持高水平专利申请
item.description: 汽车巨头Stellantis首席创新官安妮·拉利隆周二表示，尽管该公司作为战略重置的一部分取消了许多电动汽车项目，但仍计划保持高水平的专利申请。【原文：Car giant Stellantis plans to keep a ​high level of patent ‌filings despite the cancellation of many electric vehicle projects as ​part of a ​strategic reset, its chief of ⁠innovation Anne Laliron said ​on Tuesday.】

item.title: 中国网约车平台WeRide因伊朗冲突扩大暂停迪拜自动驾驶出租车服务
item.description: 中国自动驾驶企业文创出行已暂停其在迪拜的无人驾驶出租车车队运营。随着美国与以色列对伊朗冲突的升级，中东关键枢纽地区局势动荡，而这些地区正是无人驾驶出租车行业最具发展前景的市场。【原文：Chinese autonomous driving firm WeRide has suspended its robotaxi fleet in Dubai as the ​escalating U.S.-Israel conflict with Iran roiled key Middle Eastern ‌hubs that have emerged as the robotaxi industry's most promising markets.】

item.title: 丰田的收购协议对埃利奥特而言是比公司治理更大的胜利
item.description: 丰田汽车公司决定进一步提高对集团旗下丰田工业公司的收购报价，这标志着激进投资基金埃利奥特投资管理公司取得了胜利。该基金数月来一直敦促这家汽车制造商大幅提高收购价格。【原文：Toyota's decision to further sweeten its bid for group company Toyota Industries marks a win for activist fund Elliott Investment Management, ​which had pushed the automaker for months for a heftier bump up in price.】

item.title: 越南经济蓬勃发展，但外国资金正从股市撤离
item.description: 越南正站在加入新兴市场俱乐部的门槛上，股市创下数年来的最大涨幅，但外国投资者却在抛售股票，并表示关税风险、持股限制以及某家企业对指数的垄断地位都阻碍了投资。【原文：Vietnam is on the cusp of joining the emerging-markets club and stocks have notched their biggest rally in years, but foreigners have been sellers and say investing is handicapped by tariff risks, ownership limits and one firm's dominance of the index.】

item.title: 华尔街期货下跌，中东冲突加剧通胀担忧
item.description: 美国股市期货下跌，因投资者评估美以对伊朗加强打击的后续影响，并为能源价格飙升可能推高通胀的可能性做好准备。【原文：U.S. stock ​index futures fell as investors assessed the fallout of intensified U.S. and Israeli strikes ‌on Iran and braced for the possibility that a surge in energy prices could drive inflation higher.】

item.title: 美元重拾魔力——但仅因缺乏替代选择
item.description: 尽管人们很想认为，自周末伊朗袭击事件以来，美元久违的"避险"需求已然回归，但实际情况并非如此简单明了，更多是受能源板块相对表现的影响。然而，市场反应所传递的信号可能同样具有深远影响。【原文：While it's tempting to assume the dollar's long-lost "safety" bid has returned since the weekend Iran attacks, it's not as clear-cut as it seems and owes more to relative energy plays. Yet the implications of the market response may be just as powerful.】

item.title: 《金融时报》报道，贝莱德支持的财团寻求在不包含巴拿马资产的情况下完成和记黄埔港口交易
item.description: 据《金融时报》周二报道，在巴拿马当局扣押相关资产后，由贝莱德支持的财团正推动完成对和记黄埔全球港口业务的收购，但不包括巴拿马的两个码头。【原文：A BlackRock-backed consortium ​is pushing to complete its acquisition of ‌CK Hutchison's global ports business without two terminals in Panama, after authorities seized the assets, Financial Times reported ​on Tuesday.】

item.title: 海湾地区机场关闭后，亚欧航线机票价格飙升
item.description: 由于美以联军对伊朗发动战争，中东主要枢纽机场关闭，亚洲至欧洲航线机票价格飙升，多家航空公司官网显示多条热门航线机票已售罄数日。【原文：The price of flights between Asia and ​Europe has soared after the closure of key Middle Eastern hubs due to the U.S.-Israel war against Iran, with airline websites showing tickets ‌on many popular routes booked out for days.】

item.title: 《金融时报》报道称，派拉蒙以1100亿美元收购华纳兄弟的交易有望获得美国联邦通信委员会支持
item.description: 美国联邦通信委员会主席布伦丹·卡尔已表示，该监管机构不会阻止派拉蒙以1100亿美元收购华纳兄弟的交易，并淡化了CBS与CNN合并引发的竞争担忧，英国《金融时报》周二报道称。【原文：U.S. Federal Communications Commission Chair ​Brendan Carr has signaled ‌that the watchdog will not seek ​to block Paramount's $110 ​billion deal to buy ⁠Warner Bros and ​played down competition ​concerns over a combination of CBS and CNN, ​the Financial Times ​reported on Tuesday.】

item.title: 日本季度资本支出增长6.5%，政府力图刺激投资
item.description: 日本财务省周二公布的数据显示，去年第四季度日本工厂和设备支出同比增长6.5%，表明强劲的投资需求持续支撑着勉强增长的经济。【原文：Japanese spending on factories and equipment rose 6.5% ‌on-year in the fourth quarter, Ministry of Finance data showed on Tuesday, indicating resilient investment demand continued to support a barely growing economy.】

item.title: 油价上涨，因美以与伊朗冲突扩大加剧供应风险
item.description: 周二油价连续第三日上涨，因美国与以色列对伊朗的冲突不断升级，以及霍尔木兹海峡航运面临的威胁，加剧了市场对中东关键产油区供应中断的担忧。【原文：Oil prices rose for a third day on Tuesday as the widening U.S.-Israeli conflict with Iran and threats to shipping through the Strait of Hormuz heightened fears of supply disruptions from the key Middle ​East producing region.】

item.title: 据彭博社报道，Thoma Bravo即将达成收购WWEX的交易，将组建一家市值120亿美元的航运科技公司。
item.description: 彭博新闻周一援引知情人士消息报道，美国私募股权公司托马斯·布拉沃正就收购第三方物流服务商WWEX集团进行深入谈判，此举将缔造一家价值高达120亿美元的运输技术企业。【原文：U.S. private ​equity firm Thoma Bravo ‌is in advanced talks to buy third-party logistics provider ​WWEX Group to create ​a shipping technology business worth ⁠as much as $12 billion, ​Bloomberg News reported on ​Monday, citing people familiar with the matter.】

item.title: OpenAI首席执行官阿尔特曼表示，该公司正修改与五角大楼的协议。
item.description: OpenAI首席执行官山姆·阿尔特曼周一表示，这家ChatGPT开发商正与美国国防部合作，对双方协议进行部分调整。【原文：OpenAI Chief Executive Sam Altman said on Monday ​that the ChatGPT-maker is working ‌with the U.S. Department of Defense to make some changes ​in their agreement.】
